predictions.description: out-of-band: both attachment files exist on disk before the send, and the harness reads the text attachment's bytes back directly — the exact unicode payload handed to SMTP

equals: sgt matrix attachment Ünïcødé 漢字 content ✔

predictions.description: ARRANGE fetch saw the standing [sgt-calibration] email — the fetcher provably works before any zero-match claim
no message carrying this session's marker existed before ACT
the matrix message was accepted by SMTP (a real Message-ID came back) and was then found by IMAP within the poll budget — cross-protocol witness: nodemailer writes, imapflow reads

exactly ONE copy was delivered even though to, cc and bcc all name the same mailbox — the MTA de-duplicates by recipient (an environment/Gmail contract, not a StepwiseGO one; a flip to 2 is an environment finding, not a product regression)

the structured subject round-tripped byte-exact — commas, semicolons and double quotes survive SMTP encoding and the IMAP envelope decode, with no =?UTF-8?Q?...?= leakage (in-bot equalsExact pin; the DSL cannot expand {var:_sgtSessionId} inside a comparator literal)
To and Cc both carry the configured test account (captured from {svar:sgtMailAccount} at run start)

PRIVACY PIN — bcc was set on the send, and the delivered copy carries NO Bcc recipient. Independently confirmed against the raw RFC-822 headers of the delivered message (Return-Path/From/To/Cc present, Bcc absent): the MTA strips it, and bccRecipients is empty for received mail

priority survives the IMAP round trip: the message was sent with priority=high and the fetched EmailObject reports importance 'High'. GUARDS THE FIX shipped in this track — buildEmailFromImapMessage used to hard-code 'Normal' because the ENVELOPE has no priority field and the runner never fetched headers; it now fetches X-Priority / Importance / X-MSMail-Priority and maps them.

the 2-attachment message reports hasAttachments=true and attachmentCount '2' — attachments are counted by walking the BODYSTRUCTURE for parts carrying a filename, NOT by counting MIME children (the old countAttachments() returned childNodes.length, so this message reported '3' and any plain multipart/alternative mail reported hasAttachments=true; fixed in this track — this prediction guards it)

PRODUCT CONTRACT — with downloadAttachments off (the only usable setting, see the review: downloadAttachments:true DEADLOCKS the runner), the EmailObject.attachments array is never populated, so {..|attachmentNames} and {..|filePaths} are both empty strings. Attachment NAME and CONTENT fidelity are therefore not witnessable through the IMAP family today

the HTML body round-trips through IMAP. GUARDS THE FIX shipped in this track — get-emails-imap fetched envelope + bodyStructure only, so body/bodyText came back EMPTY for every message while the Graph family returned real bodies. The runner now downloads the text/plain and text/html leaves (imapflow decodes quoted-printable/base64 and transcodes to UTF-8). Structural contains-check, not byte-exact: mail servers rewrap HTML.
after the targeted delete-single-email-imap (live {sfl:loopItem} step token), a re-fetch finds zero session-marked messages — the mailbox is left exactly as ARRANGE found it
